theory.canonical_statement:
The weak-field flow dynamics are governed by a differential equation $\partial$t$\psi$=-$
abla$‚ãÖ(D(x)$
abla$\psi$)+V(x)$\psi$\partial\_t 	extbackslash psi = -
abla \cdot (D(x) 
abla 	extbackslash psi) + V(x) 	extbackslash psi$\partial$t$\psi$=-$
abla$‚ãÖ(D(x)$
abla$\psi$)+V(x)$\psi$, where D(x)D(x)D(x) is the spatially varying diffusion coefficient set by the metamaterial distribution and V(x)V(x)V(x) encodes potential gradients imposed by the strong-field meta-bits.\
theory.technical_interpretation: Technically, nodal field hamiltonian is interpreted through the variables and operators present in the canonical relation. The equation support section should preserve these source equations in sequence so later proof and Wolfram passes operate on the same mathematical object.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
\tzpsection{Direct Equation Progression}
\begin{enumerate}[leftmargin=1.6em,itemsep=7pt,topsep=4pt]
\item \textbf{Canonical Relation}
\[
The weak-field flow dynamics are governed by a differential equation $\partial$t$\psi$=-$\nabla$‚ãÖ(D(x)$\nabla$\psi$)+V(x)$\psi$\partial\_t \textbackslash psi = -\nabla \cdot (D(x) \nabla \textbackslash psi) + V(x) \textbackslash psi$\partial$t$\psi$=-$\nabla$‚ãÖ(D(x)$\nabla$\psi$)+V(x)$\psi$, where D(x)D(x)D(x) is the spatially varying diffusion coefficient set by the metamaterial distribution and V(x)V(x)V(x) encodes potential gradients imposed by the strong-field meta-bits.\\
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for nodal field hamiltonian.

\item \textbf{Support Relation}
\[
$\partial\_t \textbackslash psi = -\nabla \cdot (D(x) \nabla \textbackslash psi) + V(x) \textbackslash psi$
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for nodal field hamiltonian.

\item \textbf{Closure Relation}
\[
Component[s][Œº‚ÇÅ, Œº‚ÇÇ, d] = (3Œº‚ÇÄŒº‚ÇÅŒº‚ÇÇ)/(2œÄd‚Å¥) ‚Üí ‚àû as d‚Üí0
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for nodal field hamiltonian.

\item \textbf{Auxiliary Relation}
\[
In a superfluid or Bose--Einstein condensate, the macroscopic wavefunction $\Psi$(r,t)=n(r,t)eiœï(r,t)\Psi(r,t) = \sqrt\{n(r,t)\} e\^{}\{i\phi(r,t)\}\Psi$(r,t)=n(r,t)eiœï(r,t) exhibits circulation quantization: ‚àÆv‚ãÖd‚Ñì=2$\pi$‚Ñèn/m\textbackslash oint v \cdot d\ell = 2\textbackslash pi \hbar n/m‚àÆv‚ãÖd‚Ñì=2$\pi$‚Ñèn/m, where nnn is the integer winding number and mmm the mass of the constituent particle.\\
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for nodal field hamiltonian.

\item \textbf{Review Relation}
\[
$\partial$
\]
This relation is retained from the source-repair evidence for nodal field hamiltonian.

\end{enumerate}

\tzpsection{Proof}
The proof route is review-conditioned: verify that the listed source equations are mutually admissible in the entry's TRAWIN-normalized frame.
